note: Tiered curriculum, v3. Drops most NIST drawings from hard tier per Shef ruling 2026-04-18: NIST PMI parts are designed to test PMI-extraction software (intentionally tortuous callouts) and are too brutal as agent-evaluation inputs. Model Mania drawings are real engineering challenge problems — much fairer scoring signal. Hard tier now leads with Model Mania; one NIST kept (ctc_01) as a stretch reference. Per-iteration sample is 3 briefs (round-robin within tier). Bumping `eval_set_id` to v3 — prior scoreboard runs against eval_v2 are tagged but not directly comparable to eval_v3 means.

easy.description: Trivial procedural parts. Claude should one-shot these with the current skill; if it can't, the skill is broken.

medium.description: Envelope tier — render + bbox envelope, no GD&T. Tests shape replication without dimensional rigor. Mix of NIST and Model Mania.

hard.description: Engineering drawing tier — Model Mania challenge problems with realistic dimension callouts. NIST PMI dropped per ruling 2026-04-18 (designed for PMI software extraction tests, not LLM agent evaluation; callouts too tortuous to be informative). One NIST included (ctc_01) as a stretch.
